rows.requirement: When the catalog Lookup capability is advertised, the MCP endpoint MUST expose both `lookup_catalog` and `get_product` tools
rows.quote: When the Lookup capability is advertised, both `lookup_catalog` and `get_product` tools MUST be available.
rows.notes: Subject: MCP catalog endpoint (server). Distinct catalog-specific tool-availability obligation embedded in conformance bullet 2; parallels REST CAT-023. Server-observable. [added by completeness-2026-07-03] [completeness: not observable against the REST controlled fixture as-is; needs MCP transport fixture; tracked GAP.]

rows.requirement: The A2A business agent MUST return the checkout object inside an A2A Message DataPart keyed `a2a.ucp.checkout`
rows.quote:
The checkout
object **MUST** be returned as part of a `DataPart` object with key
`a2a.ucp.checkout`.
rows.notes: Subject: business agent (server) response format over A2A transport. Genuinely missed - no A2A/DataPart rows exist. Server-observable. [added by completeness-2026-07-03] [completeness: not observable against the REST controlled fixture as-is; needs A2A transport fixture; tracked GAP.]

rows.requirement: MCP request payloads MUST NOT carry an `id` inside the `checkout` object; the target resource is identified by a top-level `id` parameter
rows.quote:
The `checkout` object in the request payload **MUST NOT** contain
    an `id` field.
rows.notes: Subject: MCP client constructing the request (identifier-pattern well-formedness). Not directly server-observable (spec does not mandate the endpoint reject an id in checkout), so manual/client-side. Canonical id-field row for 2026-04-08 (cart-mcp restates it). [added by completeness-2026-07-03]

rows.requirement: A conforming MCP checkout endpoint MUST implement JSON-RPC 2.0, provide all core checkout tools, return errors per core spec, return business outcomes as JSON-RPC result with UCP envelope and messages array, validate tool inputs against schemas, and support HTTP streaming
rows.quote:
A conforming MCP transport implementation **MUST**:

1. Implement JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol correctly.
2. Provide all core checkout tools defined in this specification.
3. Return errors per the [Core Specification](overview.md#error-handling).
4. Return business outcomes as JSON-RPC `result` with UCP envelope and
    `messages` array.
5. Validate tool inputs against UCP schemas.
6. Support HTTP transport with streaming.
rows.notes: Subject: the MCP transport implementation (server/endpoint). Genuinely missed - register deferred the MCP binding (see cart.json note). Canonical MCP-conformance list for 2026-04-08; cart-mcp L635 and catalog-mcp L690 restate the same stem. [added by completeness-2026-07-03] [completeness: not observable against the REST controlled fixture as-is; needs MCP transport fixture; tracked GAP.]

rows.requirement: MCP transport MUST expose UCP OpenRPC operations via JSON-RPC tools/call, mapping method->params.name and params->params.arguments
rows.quote:
requires all tool invocations to use a `tools/call` method with the operation
name and arguments wrapped in `params`. Implementers **MUST** apply this
transformation:
rows.notes: Subject: implementer (server). Distinct, more granular than the JSON-RPC bullet in the conformance stem: the concrete OpenRPC->tools/call param transformation. Observable on the endpoint's tool surface. [added by completeness-2026-07-03] [completeness: not observable against the REST controlled fixture as-is; needs MCP transport fixture; tracked GAP.]

rows.requirement: A business implementing the MCP order binding MUST implement the `get_order` tool per the OpenRPC schema
rows.notes: Subject: the business (server/endpoint) under 'Businesses implementing the MCP binding'. Server-observable tool-existence obligation; genuinely missed. [added by completeness-2026-07-03] [completeness: not observable against the REST controlled fixture as-is; needs MCP transport fixture; tracked GAP.]

rows.requirement: A conforming catalog MCP transport implementation MUST implement JSON-RPC 2.0 correctly, validate tool inputs against UCP schemas, support cursor-based pagination with a default limit of 10, and return -32602 (Invalid params) for requests exceeding batch size limits (the catalog MCP conformance list beyond the Lookup-tool exposure that MCP-001 covers).
rows.quote: A conforming MCP transport implementation **MUST**: ... Implement JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol correctly. ... Validate tool inputs against UCP schemas. ... Support cursor-based pagination with default limit of 10. ... Return `-32602` (Invalid params) for requests exceeding batch size limits.
rows.notes: Umbrella lead-in for the catalog MCP conformance list (items 1,3-8). MCP-001 only captures item 2 (Lookup tools). Parallels MCP-003, which is the equivalent conformance roll-up for the checkout MCP binding. [added by completeness-2026-07-03 tight-residual re-adjudication]
